flow.name: Article Version Lifecycle
flow.description: Creates a category (required dependency for article creation), then creates a fresh article in that category, publishes it, forks it to produce a new draft, retrieves the article to confirm the draft version exists, deletes the draft version, verifies the deleted version returns 404, then deletes the article and the category as teardown — leaving the project in a clean state.

step.name: Create Category
step.notes: Article creation requires a category_id — this step satisfies that dependency. The category is created at the root level (no parent_category_id). state.createdCategoryId is used in Step 2 as category_id for the article. Teardown: Step 9 deletes this category.

step.name: Create Article
step.notes: category_id (state.createdCategoryId from Step 1) is required by the API even though the spec marks it as nullable. The article is created in draft status. state.createdArticleId is used as article_id in Steps 3–8.

step.name: Publish Article
step.notes: version_number must reference a draft version. After this call the article version transitions from draft to published. state.createdVersionNumber (captured in Step 2) is used here without quotes — it is an integer.

step.name: Fork Published Version (create draft)
step.notes: The fork endpoint has no request body. It creates a new draft version based on the currently published content. Returns 409 Conflict if a draft already exists. state.draftVersionNumber is the new draft's version_number — used in Steps 6 and 7.

step.name: Retrieve Article — Verify Draft Exists
step.notes: GET /articles/{id} returns the latest version (the draft created by the fork). Asserting data.version_number equals state.draftVersionNumber confirms the fork was applied and the article now surfaces the new draft as its current version.

step.name: Delete Draft Version
step.notes: Deletes the draft version created by the fork in Step 4. After deletion the article reverts to showing only the published version (state.publishedVersionNumber). state.deletedVersionNumber is captured from the request path param for use in Step 7.

step.name: Verify Deleted Version Returns 404
step.notes: Confirms the deleted draft version is truly gone. A 200 response here would be a test failure — the version should no longer exist after Step 6.

step.name: Delete Article (cleanup)
step.notes: teardown: true — this step always runs even if Steps 3–7 failed. The article must be deleted before the category (Step 9) because the API will reject category deletion while articles still exist in it. If Step 2 failed and state.createdArticleId is not set, this step should be skipped gracefully by the test implementation.

step.name: Delete Category (cleanup)
step.notes: teardown: true — runs after article deletion (Step 8) to clean up the category created in Step 1. Must run after Step 8 since the API requires the category to be empty before deletion. If Step 1 failed and state.createdCategoryId is not set, this step should be skipped gracefully by the test implementation.
